title: carpodetus flexuosus
intro: Carpodetus flexuosus, also known as the 'Renga Renga' or 'Makomako', is a flowering plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive, twisted branches and clusters of small, white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in New Zealand herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and bark have been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, and are said to provide health benefits such as improved wound healing and reduced inflammation. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or infusion, or used in a tincture or salve to harness its medicinal properties.
